title: Multiscale simulations of complex systems by learning their effective dynamics
methods: long short-term memory (LSTM) recurrent neural network

data_scope.origin: All data synthetically generated by authors using open-source numerical solvers; no external observational datasets
data_scope.fhn_system: N=101 spatial grid; 6 initial conditions; train 3x451 + val 2x451 + test 10000 coarse time-steps; delta_t=1 (coarse)
data_scope.ks_system: 64 spatial grid points; domain L=22; 3 independent runs x 15000 samples each; delta_t=0.25 (coarse)
data_scope.cylinder_flow: Re in {100, 1000}; state in R^{4x512x1024}; 250 train + 250 val time-steps per Reynolds number; delta_t=0.2
data_scope.hardware: Single Nvidia Tesla P100 GPU on Piz Daint supercomputer (CSCS); 12 CPU cores for reference DNS

core_conclusion: The Learning Effective Dynamics (LED) framework pairs convolutional autoencoders with LSTM recurrent neural networks to learn low-dimensional latent representations of complex system dynamics, reducing computational cost by up to two orders of magnitude while maintaining full-system prediction accuracy. A multiscale switching scheme between latent (macro) and full-resolution (micro) propagation provides a controllable accuracy-speed trade-off. Validated on FitzHugh-Nagumo, Kuramoto-Sivashinsky, and viscous cylinder flow benchmarks, LED outperforms state-of-the-art reduced-order models and equation-free variants across all systems.
why_it_matters: Breaks the cost barrier of multiscale scientific simulation by 100x; systematic data-driven framework replacing heuristic closures; continuous user-tuneable accuracy-speed trade-off via multiscale ratio rho; demonstrated applicability from chemistry to fluid mechanics; fully open-source (cselab/LED on GitHub).
why_nature: Algorithmic novelty at the ML-computational science intersection with cross-disciplinary reach; rigorous benchmark-grounded quantitative claims with reproducible hyperparameter tables; advances the ML-for-science agenda by replacing simulation itself rather than post-processing; resolves a 20-year-old open problem in the equation-free framework.
added_value_to_my_research: Resolution-invariant LED encoder via neural operator backbone -> generalizable geospatial foundation model across heterogeneous urban grid resolutions
LED AE+LSTM as LES subgrid-scale closure -> 100x cheaper city-scale CFD for urban health-exposure and heat-island modeling
LED latent emulator for OD flow matrices -> fast mobility scenario simulator for R2SFCA dynamic demand accessibility
Adaptive meta-controller for rho -> uncertainty-aware self-tuning resolution for Monte Carlo / SIR epidemic simulations
LED intrinsic latent manifold as ante-hoc explainability -> physics-grounded feature space complementing SHAP/MGWR in GeoAI health modeling

related.title: Deep neural operator for free boundary problems
related.relation: neural operator replaces grid-fixed CNN-AE to enable resolution-invariant LED encoder

related.title: Automating turbulence modelling by multi-agent reinforcement learning
related.relation: learned subgrid-scale closure parallels LED's equation-free turbulence extension

related.title: Enhancing computational fluid dynamics with machine learning
related.relation: ML augmentation of CFD solvers complements LED's cylinder-flow benchmark application

related.title: Machine learning solutions looking for PDE problems
related.relation: survey of ML approaches to PDEs provides context for LED's equation-free framework extension

related.title: A machine learning-based multiscale model to predict bone formation in scaffolds
related.relation: parallel ML-based multiscale modelling in a different scientific domain (biomechanics)

figures.caption: Multiscale-LED schematic — warm-up via micro-solver feeds AE encoder; LSTM propagates latent dynamics for T_m; decoder lifts back; micro-solver corrects for T_mu, then repeats
FitzHugh-Nagumo benchmark — latent-space attractor fidelity, inhibitor density predictions, MNAD vs. rho, 60x speed-up over LB solver at Latent-LED
Kuramoto-Sivashinsky benchmark — latent dimension selection (plateau at d_z=8), phase-space density reproduction, MNAD and correlation vs. rho, up to 100x speed-up
Latent forecasting — vorticity field prediction for Re=100 and Re=1000 at lead time T=4, MNAD vs. lead time, latent-space attractor fidelity over T=20 horizon
Multiscale-LED for cylinder flow — propagator comparison (LSTM vs. SINDy vs. RC), MNAD and drag error vs. rho, 100x speed-up for Re=100 and Re=1000
